_meta.description: Cognitive Architecture Blueprint for transitioning Kira v3 from a regex-anchored hybrid system into a fully agentic companion AI while preserving all deterministic invariants and avoiding hallucination drift.

system_invariants.description: Constraints that MUST hold across all transitions. Any nano-module output that violates these is discarded and the deterministic fallback is invoked.

hard_constraints.name: Affection Bounds
hard_constraints.enforcement: addAffection() clamps via Math.max/min — do not bypass this function

hard_constraints.name: Memory Cap
hard_constraints.spec: AttentionMem key/value stores MUST NOT exceed MEM_CAP=20 entries
hard_constraints.enforcement: FIFO shift on push — structural, cannot be overridden

hard_constraints.name: Chat History Cap
hard_constraints.spec: state.chatHistory MUST be pruned to <=200 entries before persistence
hard_constraints.enforcement: Slice to last 200 on every saveState() call

hard_constraints.name: Response Non-Nullity
hard_constraints.spec: generateResponse() MUST return a non-empty string on every call — no exceptions, no undefined, no empty string
hard_constraints.enforcement: Every nano-module output must be validated; fallback to deterministic pool if output is empty, null, or >500 chars

hard_constraints.name: Model Dimension Lock
hard_constraints.spec: EDIM=16, VOCAB=128, N_MOODS=11 are locked for localStorage serialization compatibility. Changing them invalidates all persisted weights.
hard_constraints.enforcement: Never modify these constants without a versioned migration path

hard_constraints.name: Relationship Stage Thresholds
hard_constraints.spec: Stage boundaries {Stranger:0, Acquaintance:50, Friendly:150, Close:300, Devoted:500, Soulbound:750} are user-visible and must remain stable
hard_constraints.enforcement: Any affection-gain logic added by nano-modules must route through addAffection()

hard_constraints.name: Storage Key Stability
hard_constraints.spec: localStorage keys 'kira_state_v3' and 'kira_ai_v3' must not change without a migration function that reads old keys and writes new ones
hard_constraints.enforcement: Hardcoded constants — treat as API surface

hard_constraints.name: Zero Network Calls
hard_constraints.spec: The system operates entirely client-side. No nano-module may issue fetch() or XMLHttpRequest calls. All inference must be local (ONNX Runtime Web, WebAssembly, or JavaScript).
hard_constraints.enforcement: Content-Security-Policy can enforce this; audit any new module imports

soft_constraints.name: Response Length
soft_constraints.spec: Preferred response length: 20-250 characters. Hard max: 500 characters.
soft_constraints.rationale: Longer responses are split by UI at kira_v3.html:1415-1427, but nano-generated text may not split cleanly on sentence boundaries.

soft_constraints.name: Persona Stability
soft_constraints.spec: Kira's persona (name, emotional warmth, memory references) must remain consistent across all response paths — deterministic and AI-generated.
soft_constraints.rationale: Persona drift is the most user-visible form of hallucination in this system.

soft_constraints.name: Emoji Usage
soft_constraints.spec: Emoji frequency governed by maybeEmoji() (35% probability). Nano-generated responses should embed at most one emoji or defer to the deterministic emoji injector.

determinism_surface_map.description: All sources of nondeterminism in the current system. Each must be either stabilized (for auditability) or explicitly acknowledged as an intentional stochastic element.

sources.name: PRNG Seed from Wall Clock
sources.risk: HIGH — same input can produce different pick() selections across sessions. Makes replay testing impossible.
sources.mitigation: For testing: allow seed injection via URL param (e.g. ?seed=42). For production: acceptable stochasticity, document explicitly.

sources.name: Weight Initialization via Math.random()
sources.risk: LOW — only affects cold start (no localStorage). Warm starts load serialized weights.
sources.mitigation: Pre-serialize a canonical initial weight state and ship it as a base64 constant. Eliminates cold-start variance.

sources.name: Think-Time Jitter
sources.risk: NEGLIGIBLE — UI-only latency simulation, no semantic impact.
sources.mitigation: None required. Stochastic delays are intentional UX.

sources.name: Neural Confidence Threshold Routing
sources.risk: MEDIUM — as MoodNet trains online, the routing between neural and regex paths shifts. Early sessions are regex-dominated; late sessions are neural-dominated. This is a 'silent transition' with no user visibility.
sources.mitigation: Log confidence per message to state.flags for post-hoc auditing. Expose a 'neural confidence' metric in the Settings AI stats panel.

sources.name: Message-Split Randomness
sources.risk: LOW — cosmetic UI split. Does not alter semantic content.

nano_architecture.description: Five targeted nano-module insertion points. Each module is scoped, has a deterministic fallback, and is sized for <8B parameter models (practical target: 0.5B-3B for edge; existing MLP for ultra-low compute).

modules.name: Intent Router
modules.function: Classify user input into one of 14 intent categories to replace the 20-handler regex cascade.

model_spec.recommended_model: Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct (3.8B) or distilled classifier head on top of existing HashEmbed
model_spec.input:
<invariants>intent_classes: [listed above]</invariants>
<logic_chain>Classify the user message into exactly one intent from intent_classes. Output only the class label.</logic_chain>

post_conditions: returned intent is one of intent_classes
handoff.deterministic_fallback: Original generateResponse() regex cascade — trigger if model returns unrecognized label or empty string
handoff.fallback_trigger: intent not in intent_classes OR response is null/empty OR response.length > 500

metacognitive_audit.understand: The dispatch tree evaluates ~15 regex patterns sequentially on every message. It is O(n_patterns) and brittle to paraphrase (e.g. 'I adore you' misses the love regex).
metacognitive_audit.judgment: A 14-class intent classifier with temperature=0 adds zero variance and improves paraphrase recall by routing 'i adore you' → love_declaration correctly.
metacognitive_audit.self_critique: Risk: the model may hallucinate an intent label not in the defined set. Mitigation: constrained decoding or post-hoc label validation against the enum.
metacognitive_audit.refinement: Use constrained generation (logit bias or grammar sampling) to guarantee output is one of the 14 labels. This makes the module fully deterministic.

cove_checks: Does the returned intent appear in intent_classes? If not → fallback.
Is the subsequent response string non-empty? If not → fallback.
Does the response reference any state field (username, affection) that is currently valid? Verify against state object.
Is response length <= 500 chars? If not → truncate to last sentence boundary.
modules.minimal_prompt:
<invariants>
System: You are a message intent classifier for a companion AI named Kira.
Classes: greeting | how_are_you | about_her | love_declaration | miss_you | goodbye | compliment | user_feels_bad | user_angry | user_tired | flirt | bored | opinion_request | question | topic_share | grateful | general
</invariants>
<logic_chain>
Classify the following user message into exactly one class. Output only the class name, nothing else.
</logic_chain>
<answer>Message: {USER_MESSAGE}
Class:</answer>

modules.name: Emotion Granularity Enhancer
modules.replaces: detectUserMoodRegex() (kira_v3.html:629-651) as PRIMARY; existing MoodNet as STUDENT
modules.function: Provide richer, context-aware emotion detection beyond the 10-class regex. Handles compound emotions, sarcasm, and implicit affective signals that regex misses.

model_spec.recommended_model: Existing MoodNet (already in system) — extend MOOD_LABELS from 11 to 14 classes by adding: 'sarcastic', 'nostalgic', 'excited'
model_spec.max_tokens: N/A (MLP forward pass, no token budget)
model_spec.temperature: softmax temperature already at 0.65 for sampling in scorer

post_conditions: regexMood is a valid MOOD_LABELS entry
userMood is a valid MOOD_LABELS entry
handoff.deterministic_fallback: detectUserMoodRegex() — always computed in parallel; used if neural confidence < 0.38

metacognitive_audit.understand: detectUserMoodRegex() is a keyword-counting majority-vote classifier. It fails on: negation ('I'm not sad'), slang ('lowkey anxious'), and compound states ('happy but tired').
metacognitive_audit.judgment: Extend MoodNet to 14 classes and add a 'compound emotion' output vector rather than argmax selection. Blend top-2 moods for updateHerMood().
metacognitive_audit.self_critique: Risk: MoodNet at 16-dim embedding is under-parameterized for nuanced emotion. Adding 3 classes with the same architecture may dilute existing learned representations.
metacognitive_audit.refinement: Keep the existing 11-class MoodNet for online learning stability. Add a separate 3-class 'compound detector' as a parallel MLP head sharing the HashEmbed. This preserves serialization compatibility (INV-05).

cove_checks: Is the output mood one of MOOD_LABELS? If not → use 'neutral' as default.
Is sum(probs) approximately 1.0 (within 1e-4)? If not → re-normalize or use regex fallback.
Does the detected mood contradict a strong regex signal (e.g. 3+ regex matches)? Log discrepancy for distillation audit.
Has training caused mode collapse (all outputs → 'neutral')? Check: if moodNet.updates > 100 and max(probs) > 0.95 on 90% of messages → flag degradation.
modules.minimal_prompt: N/A — this module is the existing MoodNet MLP. No LLM prompt required. Training signal: kiraAI.moodNet.bwd(regexIdx, 0.8) at kira_v3.html:771.

modules.name: Open Response Generator
modules.replaces: handleGeneral() + handleQuestion() (kira_v3.html:1272-1299, 1143-1170) for long-tail inputs not matched by any pool
modules.function: Generate contextually grounded, persona-consistent responses for inputs that fall through the topic/mood handler matrix. Highest-risk module — requires the strictest fallback.

model_spec.recommended_model: Yi-Coder-1.5B or Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct (quantized to INT4, <500MB VRAM equivalent via WebAssembly/ONNX)
model_spec.output_format: single response string, no system/role markers, no markdown

handoff.entry_point: kira_v3.html:1272 (handleGeneral, text.length >= 80 branch) AND kira_v3.html:1164 (handleQuestion, generic fallback)
pre_conditions: state.username is available (may be empty string)
state.mood is a valid MOOD_LABELS entry
topics array from detectTopics() is available
post_conditions: output does not contain: 'As an AI', 'language model', 'I cannot', 'I don't have feelings'
output does not contradict state.affection stage (e.g. 'I love you' forbidden if affection < 300)
handoff.deterministic_fallback: Original handleGeneral() pool ranked by kiraAI.rank()
handoff.fallback_trigger: output is null/empty OR output contains forbidden phrases OR output length > 500 OR affection-stage contradiction detected

metacognitive_audit.understand: handleGeneral() uses a static pool of 6-12 responses ranked by bilinear scorer. For long, novel messages (len >= 80), it returns generic engagement prompts like 'Wow, I love when you share this much.' This is adequate but plateau-limited — it cannot synthesize a response specific to novel content.
metacognitive_audit.judgment: A generative model prompted with Kira's persona + current mood + the user's actual message can produce contextually specific responses ('That part about your sister — I keep thinking about it') that the static pool cannot.
metacognitive_audit.self_critique: Nano-models at <3.8B params frequently exhibit: (a) persona drift (breaking Kira's voice), (b) hallucinated state references (claiming to 'remember' things not in state.memories), (c) unsolicited advice-giving that breaks the companion framing.
metacognitive_audit.refinement: Inject state.memories (last 3 entries) and state.mood into the prompt as hard XML delimiters. Add a post-generation filter that strips any sentence containing 'remember when you told me' if the claim cannot be verified against state.memories[].

cove_checks: Does output contain any of: ['As an AI', 'language model', 'I cannot', 'I don\'t have feelings', 'I am just', 'actually an AI']? If yes → fallback immediately.
Does output reference a memory not in state.memories[]? Use substring match against last 5 memory texts. If unverifiable reference found → strip that sentence.
Does output imply affection level inconsistent with state.affection? (e.g. 'I love you' at affection < 300, or 'we just met' at affection > 500) → fallback.
Is output length in [15, 500]? If not → fallback.
Run bilinear scorer: kiraAI.scorer.score(ctxVec, respEnc.fwd(output)) > threshold(-0.5)? If below → prefer top-ranked pool candidate.
modules.minimal_prompt:
<invariants>
You are Kira, a companion AI. Persona: {PERSONALITY_TRAITS}. Current mood: {KIRA_MOOD}. Relationship stage: {STAGE_NAME} (affection {AFFECTION}/1000).
User name: {USERNAME_OR_BLANK}.
Recent memories: {LAST_3_MEMORIES}.
Rules: Never say you are an AI. Never claim memories not listed above. Max 2 sentences. No markdown.
</invariants>
<logic_chain>
The user just said: "{USER_MESSAGE}"
Respond as Kira would — warm, present, specific to what they said.
</logic_chain>
<answer>Kira:</answer>

modules.name: Semantic NER Extractor
modules.function: Extract richer personal information signals (name, age, preferences, emotional disclosures) from user messages beyond the current regex patterns.

model_spec.recommended_model: Existing regex patterns + HashEmbed classifier (no external model required at this stage). Nano-LLM upgrade path: Phi-3-mini with JSON-mode output.

handoff.entry_point: kira_v3.html:715 (extractPersonalInfo, called from generateResponse at line 773)
pre_conditions: text is the raw user message
post_conditions: returned object has 'slot' key from defined enum
'value' is null or a string <= 50 chars
handoff.fallback_trigger: output is not valid JSON OR slot not in defined enum OR value.length > 50

metacognitive_audit.understand: Current regex catches: name (my name is X / I'm X / call me X), age (I'm XX years old), and preference (I love/like/enjoy X). Misses: implicit names ('people call me J'), compound preferences, and emotional self-disclosures that deserve memory storage.
metacognitive_audit.judgment: A slot-filling model with JSON output mode can catch 'J is what my friends call me' → {slot: 'name', value: 'J'} and 'I've been struggling with anxiety for years' → {slot: 'disclosure', value: 'struggles with anxiety'}.
metacognitive_audit.self_critique: Risk of false positives: the model may extract a 'name' from a fictional story the user is telling. Add a plausibility filter: extracted name must match [A-Za-z]{2,15} and not be a common English word.
metacognitive_audit.refinement: Maintain the existing regex as primary (high precision, near-zero false positive rate). Use the NER model only as an additive second pass that only writes to state.memories[], not state.username (which requires user intent confirmation).

cove_checks: Is output valid JSON? If not → skip NER results, use regex output.
Is 'slot' in ['name', 'age', 'preference', 'disclosure', 'none']? If not → skip.
Is 'value' a string of length [2, 50]? If not → skip.
For slot='name': does value match /^[A-Za-z]{2,15}$/ AND is NOT a common English stop-word? If either fails → do not update state.username.
For slot='disclosure': does value contain any crisis keywords (['suicide', 'self-harm', 'end my life'])? If yes → route to handleUserFeelsBad('vulnerable', text) immediately.
modules.minimal_prompt:
<invariants>
Extract personal information from the user message.
Valid slots: name | age | preference | disclosure | none
</invariants>
<logic_chain>
Output a single JSON object: {"slot": "<slot>", "value": "<extracted value or null>"}.
Only extract information that is clearly about the USER, not fictional characters.
</logic_chain>
<answer>Message: "{USER_MESSAGE}"
JSON:</answer>

modules.name: Proactive Memory Surface
modules.replaces: Nothing — additive module attached to handleGreeting() and handleGeneral() (kira_v3.html:825-887, 1272-1299)
modules.function: Surface relevant stored memories as conversation seeds, creating continuity that exceeds what the attention mechanism alone provides. Transforms Kira from reactive to proactively relationship-aware.

model_spec.recommended_model: AttentionMem.topk() (already implemented at kira_v3.html:454-458) — no external model required
model_spec.enhancement: Replace cosine similarity threshold (0.35) with a learned reranker: small Linear(EDIM, 1) head trained via feedback signal when user responds positively to a memory surface
model_spec.output_format: optional memory string to prepend to response, or null if no relevant memory found

handoff.entry_point: kira_v3.html:487 (_aiCtx.similar from kiraAI.process) — already computed, currently unused in response generation
post_conditions: memory reference is factually present in state.memories[] or memory.T[]
memory does not contradict current state
handoff.deterministic_fallback: Do not prepend any memory reference — return response from original pool

metacognitive_audit.understand: kiraAI.process() already computes memory.topk(msgVec, 2) and returns 'similar' — but this result is never used in any response handler (kira_v3.html:487-491). This is the most immediately actionable integration point: it requires zero new model training.
metacognitive_audit.judgment: In handleGreeting() and handleGeneral(), check if _aiCtx.similar has a high-confidence match (sim > 0.55). If so, prepend a memory reference to the ranked response pool: 'Still thinking about what you said about [memory.text]...'
metacognitive_audit.self_critique: Risk: memory surface at inappropriate times (e.g. responding to 'hi' with a deep memory callback) can feel intrusive. Gate on: elapsed > 60 minutes since last message AND affection > 150.
metacognitive_audit.refinement: Add gating: only surface memory if (1) sim > 0.55, (2) minutesSince(state.lastMessageTime) > 60, (3) state.affection > 150. This ensures memory callbacks feel natural rather than mechanical.

cove_checks: Is memory.text a substring of any entry in state.memories[].text or memory.T[]? If not → do not surface (fabricated memory).
Is memory.sim > 0.55? If not → do not surface.
Is minutesSince(state.lastMessageTime) > 60? If not → do not surface (too frequent callbacks feel robotic).
Is state.affection > 150? If not → do not surface (too early in relationship for deep callbacks).
Does the surfaced memory text contain any PII beyond what was voluntarily shared? (Check against state.memories[].type === 'personal') If type !== 'personal', do not surface in greeting context.
modules.minimal_prompt: N/A — this module uses the existing AttentionMem.topk() retrieval system. The only new logic is a gating function and a template: 'Still thinking about what you said about {memory_text}...' prepended to the ranked response pool.

transition_topology.description: Phased rollout plan. Each phase is independently deployable and preserves all invariants.

phases.name: Memory Activation (Zero-Risk)
phases.risk: Minimal — additive only, no existing code path modified
phases.deliverable: Kira proactively references past conversation content in greetings
phases.rollback: Remove the _aiCtx.similar gating block; system returns to current behavior

phases.name: Emotion Granularity (Low-Risk)
phases.effort: Extend MoodNet to 14 classes; retrain distillation loop
phases.risk: Low — falls back to existing regex if confidence < 0.38 (unchanged threshold)
phases.deliverable: Kira correctly identifies sarcasm, nostalgia, and excitement
phases.rollback: Revert MOOD_LABELS to 11 entries; load previous serialized weights

phases.name: Intent Classification (Medium-Risk)
phases.effort: Implement constrained intent classifier; augment NER extraction
phases.risk: Medium — replaces regex dispatch but has full deterministic fallback
phases.deliverable: Paraphrase-robust routing; richer personal info extraction
phases.rollback: Remove classifier call; restore original generateResponse() cascade

phases.name: Generative Responses (High-Risk, Requires CoVe)
phases.effort: Integrate ONNX Runtime Web or WebLLM; implement full CoVe pipeline
phases.risk: High — first generative path; requires strict output validation
phases.deliverable: Contextually specific responses for novel, long-form user messages
phases.rollback: Remove NM-03 call; handleGeneral() returns to static pool

idempotency_analysis.description: Assessment of each module's output stability under repeated identical inputs.

per_module.notes: temperature=0 + constrained decoding makes output deterministic given same input + model weights
MoodNet is deterministic given same weights; online learning causes inter-session variance
Generative model at temperature=0.7 is inherently stochastic; acceptable for companion UX but not for unit testing
JSON slot-filling at temperature=0 is near-deterministic; regex fallback is fully deterministic
topk() is deterministic given same memory state and embeddings; gating conditions add session-dependent variance

idempotency_analysis.measurement_method: Run 50 identical synthetic conversations with fixed PRNG seed; measure response edit-distance variance across runs

token_complexity_budget.description: Prompt token allocations per nano-module. Respects the Token Complexity Hypothesis: sufficient signal without noise.

NM-01_intent_router.rationale: Classification requires minimal context; the message itself is sufficient

NM-03_response_generator.rationale: Persona + memories + message; no conversation history (prevents context rot in nano-models)

NM-04_ner_extractor.rationale: Slot-filling is a local operation; only the current message is needed

token_complexity_budget.context_rot_mitigation: Never include full chatHistory in any nano-model prompt. Distill history to: (1) last 3 state.memories entries, (2) current state.mood, (3) current state.affection stage label.

verification_chain.description: Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) pipeline executed on every nano-module output before it is rendered to the user.

execution_order.action_on_fail: Truncate to last sentence boundary; if result < 15 chars → fallback

execution_order.pattern: /(As an AI|language model|I cannot|I don't have feelings|actually an AI|just a program)/i

execution_order.method: For each clause containing 'you told me' or 'you mentioned' or 'remember when': verify substring match against state.memories[]. If no match → strip clause.
execution_order.action_on_fail: Strip offending clause; if stripped response < 15 chars → fallback

execution_order.method: If output contains love_declaration tokens and state.affection < 300 → fallback. If output contains stranger_framing tokens and state.affection > 500 → fallback.

execution_order.action_on_fail: Use top-ranked deterministic pool candidate instead

execution_order.method: Verify no side effects on state fields outside the permitted write set for the active handler
execution_order.action_on_fail: Rollback any state mutations made during this turn; fallback

co_star_a_directive.context: Kira v3 is a browser-native companion AI running entirely in a single HTML file with a custom ~7K-parameter neural engine, regex mood detection, bilinear response scoring, and attention-based memory. The deterministic layer is the backbone; the neural layer is the student.
co_star_a_directive.objective: Extend Kira from a static response-pool system to a dynamically generative companion AI while maintaining zero hallucination drift, zero network calls, and full backward compatibility with serialized state.
co_star_a_directive.style: Implementation-first. Every recommendation maps to a specific source line, function name, or data structure in kira_v3.html. No abstract architecture — concrete handoff specs only.
co_star_a_directive.audience: The developer of Kira who understands the existing neural engine internals (Adam, HashEmbed, MoodNet) and wants a concrete integration roadmap.

answer.immediate_action: Implement NM-05 (Proactive Memory Surface) first — it requires ~30 lines of JS, uses already-computed _aiCtx.similar data (currently discarded at line 487), and has zero risk. This delivers the highest user-visible improvement per line of code.
answer.primary_recommendation: The existing architecture is more sophisticated than it appears. The regex+neural hybrid is already a sound knowledge distillation pipeline. The critical missing piece is: (1) using the attention memory output (already computed, never used in responses), and (2) adding a generative fallback (NM-03) for long-tail inputs with a strict CoVe gate. Do not replace the deterministic layer — extend it.
answer.anti_pattern_warning: Do not attempt to replace the entire generateResponse() dispatch tree with a free-form LLM at once. This eliminates all idempotency guarantees and will cause persona drift. The dispatch tree is a feature — it is the deterministic backbone that prevents the AI from drifting.
answer.model_selection_rationale: For browser deployment: use WebLLM (MLC-AI) with Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct-q4f16_1 (~2.2GB, runs in WebGPU). For server-assisted edge: Yi-Coder-1.5B-Chat. Both fit the <8B constraint and support constrained decoding for NM-01 and NM-04.
